title: Add user_preferences JSONB column to users table
description: Adds a nullable user_preferences column to store per-user feature flags and UI settings without requiring a separate table.
diff:
diff --git a/alembic/versions/0042_add_user_preferences.py b/alembic/versions/0042_add_user_preferences.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..aaabbbc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/alembic/versions/0042_add_user_preferences.py
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+"""Add user_preferences column.
+
+Revision ID: 0042
+Revises: 0041
+Create Date: 2026-05-01
+"""
+from alembic import op
+import sqlalchemy as sa
+from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import JSONB
+
+revision = '0042'
+down_revision = '0041'
+
+
+def upgrade() -> None:
+    op.add_column(
+        'users',
+        sa.Column(
+            'user_preferences',
+            JSONB,
+            nullable=True,
+            server_default=None,
+        ),
+    )
+
+
+def downgrade() -> None:
+    op.drop_column('users', 'user_preferences')
diff --git a/src/models/user.py b/src/models/user.py
index 3333333..4444444 100644
--- a/src/models/user.py
+++ b/src/models/user.py
@@ -12,6 +12,7 @@ class User(Base):
     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
     email: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), unique=True)
     created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(default=datetime.utcnow)
+    user_preferences: Mapped[dict | None] = mapped_column(JSONB, nullable=True)
human_notes: Adds a nullable JSONB column via Alembic. Migration is reversible. Briefing should note this is the 42nd migration on the users table and ask about concurrent access during migration. Key questions: has this been tested on a large users table without a lock? Are there existing rows that need default values populated? Does any application code read user_preferences and need a null guard?
